'''Murk''' was a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] located in the [[Murk system/Legends|Murk system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell subsector]]. It was an [[Atmosphere/Legends|airless]], barren rock with low [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] established the [[Sanctuary Pipeline/Legends|Sanctuary Pipeline]], a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] meant for the [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] of construction materials and components to the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], the Murk [[Star system/Legends|system]] became the penultimate stop on the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route.

On the planetoid, an Imperial [[cargo-transfer station]] was established, where cargoes arriving via the Sanctuary Pipeline were unloaded for subsequent shipment to the Endor system. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the [[Shell Hutts|Shell Hutt]] [[Tagoonta]] escaped to the Murk transfer station from troubles in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].

==Description==
Murk was a terrestrial [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]]<ref name="Endor" /> located within the [[Murk system/Legends|Murk system]]. It lay within the [[Outer Moddell systems]] area of<ref name="TEA Murk" /> the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell subsector]],<ref name="EAOC Murk" /> one of the [[Sector/Legends|subsectors]] making up the [[Inner Zuma Region]]<ref name="TEA Murk" /> within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Zuma sector/Legends|Zuma sector]].<ref name="EAOC Murk" />

The innermost planetoid of its [[Star system/Legends|star system]], Murk had a rotation period of eleven [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]] and took ninety-eight [[Local day/Legends|local days]] to complete an [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around its [[Star/Legends|sun]], the [[Red dwarf/Legends|red dwarf]]–type star of the [[Murk (star)|same name]]. Murk was an [[Atmosphere/Legends|airless]], [[Temperature/Legends|frigid]], barren rock, the [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] of which was fifty percent of standard.<ref name="Endor" />

==History==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] began the construction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] over the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]]. To allow for expeditious—and secret—[[Transport/Legends|transportation]] of the necessary materials and components to Endor, the Empire [[S-thread booster/Legends|artificially]] extended the [[Silvestri Trace]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] from<ref name="Endor" /> the [[Brema sector/Legends|Brema sector]]<ref name="EAOC Sullust">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]]</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] directly to Endor,<ref name="Endor" /> with the Murk system becoming a penultimate stop on the new [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route, known as the [[Sanctuary Pipeline/Legends|Sanctuary Pipeline]].<ref name="TEA Murk" />

[[File:Murk-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Freighters delivering cargo to the Murk system were unloaded on Murk.]]
To add another layer of secrecy to the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] project, an Imperial [[cargo-transfer station]] was established on Murk, used to unload shipments arriving via the Sanctuary Pipeline for further transportation to the Endor system. Following the Imperial defeat by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="Endor" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the Sanctuary Pipeline became largely [[Navigator/Legends|unnavigable]], and the Murk transfer station was abandoned. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[Shell Hutts|Shell Hutt]] [[Tagoonta]] and his retainers found refuge at the transfer station from troubles in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].<ref name="Endor" />

==Inhabitants==
Prior to the Battle of Endor, Imperial crews stationed at the Murk cargo-transfer station unloaded cargoes of raw materials and components from deep-[[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Freighter/Legends|haulers]] and [[superfreighter]]s traveling along the Sanctuary Pipeline. Following the abandonment of the transfer station, it became inhabited by the band of retainers of the Shell [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] Tagoonta, and exact data on Murk's population was not available during the New Republic's rule.<ref name="Endor" />

==Locations==
An Imperial cargo-transfer station was located on Murk, being equipped with a landing field and having facilities both on the planetoid's surface and in its orbit. Following the Battle of Endor, several of the transfer station's more valuable components were looted by [[Scavenger/Legends|scavengers]].<ref name="Endor" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planetoid Murk was first mentioned in the article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]," which was authored by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Daniel Wallace]], and [[Jason Fry]], and was published in [[Star Wars Gamer 9|''Star Wars Gamer'' 9]]<ref name="Endor" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Murk system, and therefore the planetoid, in grid square H-16.<ref name="TEA Murk" />
